error_message: pytest collection failed with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'sse_starlette' when run outside the backend uv environment
root_cause: The shell Python did not have backend dependencies installed; the project dependencies are available through the uv-managed backend environment.
fix: Use `rtk uv run pytest ...` from backend/ for backend tests.

error_message: GET /agents in Vite dev returned index.html instead of JSON
root_cause: The Vite dev proxy listed /projects, /runs, and /run, but not /agents, so the request fell through to the SPA fallback.
fix: Added /agents to the Vite proxy table.

error_message: Codex health tests ignored monkeypatched _CODEX_HOME and read the real ~/.codex account
root_cause: _codex_account used _CODEX_HOME as a default argument, so Python captured the original path at function definition time.
fix: Changed the function default to None and resolved _CODEX_HOME inside the function body.

error_message: Swift test targets failed because XCTest and Swift Testing modules were unavailable in the Command Line Tools environment
root_cause: The active developer directory is Command Line Tools, not full Xcode, so standard Swift test runtime modules were not importable.
fix: Removed the SwiftPM test target and added `Trinity --self-test` mode for built-in regression checks.

error_message: Visual verification capture failed: screencapture returned 'could not create image from display' and osascript was not allowed assistive access
root_cause: The shell session cannot capture the current display and lacks macOS Assistive Access permission for System Events, even though the Trinity release app process is running frontmost.
fix: Do not treat this as an app regression; verify Swift UI changes with compile, self-test, release bundle launch, and Computer Use app listing unless Accessibility/capture permission is granted.

error_message: Shell.run hangs / deadlocks on agent CLI output larger than the OS pipe buffer (~64KB)
root_cause: readDataToEndOfFile() was called on both pipes only AFTER process.waitUntilExit(). When the child writes more than the pipe buffer it blocks on write, while the parent blocks on waitUntilExit, deadlocking. Self-tests use a FakeShell so a real Process was never exercised.
fix: Extracted a synchronous runSync helper and drained stdout/stderr concurrently on a background queue (DispatchGroup) while the process runs; wait on the group after waitUntilExit. Added 'shell drains large output without deadlock' self-test (200KB payload).

error_message: Codex reviewer ran without --output-schema, so verdict JSON was unconstrained and prone to VerdictParser failures
root_cause: AgentCommandBuilder.build supported a schemaPath argument (and SelfTests asserted it), but AgentRunner.run never accepted or passed one, so production codex reviewer commands always omitted the schema. The schema also only existed under backend/, never shipped as a Swift resource.
fix: Copied review.schema.json into Sources/Trinity/Resources/, added AgentRunner.reviewSchemaPath() resolving it via Bundle.module (nil -> graceful degradation), and passed it through to AgentCommandBuilder.build. Added a self-test asserting the schema resolves and reaches the codex command.

error_message: slugify could keep non-ASCII digits in git branch names
root_cause: Operator precedence: 'char.isASCII && char.isLetter || char.isNumber' parses as '(isASCII && isLetter) || isNumber', so any Unicode digit (e.g. Arabic-Indic) was preserved instead of being replaced with '-'.
fix: Parenthesized to 'char.isASCII && (char.isLetter || char.isNumber)'. Added 'git slugify drops non-ascii digits' self-test.

error_message: Codex quota always blank and codex account showed a GUID instead of email in the status panel
root_cause: Both parsers used a guessed codex schema. Real on-disk data: rate_limits is nested under payload (not top-level), windows are primary/secondary (not primary_window/secondary_window), use window_minutes (not limit_window_seconds) and resets_at (not reset_at). Account email + plan live in the tokens.id_token JWT, not as plain fields; only account_id GUID was read. Unit tests passed because they encoded the same wrong schema.
fix: Decode tokens.id_token JWT (base64url) for email + https://api.openai.com/auth.chatgpt_plan_type. Read payload.rate_limits with primary/secondary, window_minutes (>=1440 => weekly), resets_at, plan_type. Fixed in both backend and Swift; rewrote tests to the real schema and added a JWT-decode self-test. Verified live against real ~/.codex on disk.

error_message: Distributed .app reports every agent CLI as 'missing - not found on PATH' even when installed
root_cause: A GUI .app launched from Finder inherits only a minimal PATH (/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin). Shell.which read that PATH and only used its hardcoded fallback when PATH was entirely ABSENT (never, for a GUI app), so Homebrew (/opt/homebrew/bin) and /usr/local/bin were not searched. Worse, AgentRunner spawns `/usr/bin/env <cli>` which resolves via the inherited minimal PATH too, so even a located CLI would fail to exec.
fix: Added Shell.augmentedPATH() = current PATH + common install dirs (/opt/homebrew/bin, /usr/local/bin, ~/.local/bin, ~/.bun, ~/.deno, ~/.cargo, etc.), deduped. which() now searches it, and runSync() sets process.environment PATH to it so `env <cli>` resolves. Added a self-test simulating a minimal Finder PATH.

error_message: Agy status showed 'Not installed' even though Antigravity.app was installed/running and live account/quota was available
root_cause: The health model treated Antigravity as installed only when the headless `agy` CLI resolved on PATH. On this machine the ~/.antigravity symlink exists but is broken, while /Applications/Antigravity.app and its language server are available for account/quota. UI conflated app availability with runnable CLI availability.
fix: Added Antigravity app detection, a separate `app` status label, an Open button that launches Antigravity, live account/quota display even without CLI, and a run-readiness warning/guard when selected roles require a missing CLI. Also added ~/.antigravity/antigravity/bin to the augmented PATH for installs where the symlink is valid.

error_message: swift build failed: missing argument for parameter 'format' in PropertyListSerialization.propertyList call
root_cause: The Antigravity app version reader used `PropertyListSerialization.propertyList(from:)`, but Swift's Foundation API requires `propertyList(from:options:format:)`.
fix: Changed the call to `PropertyListSerialization.propertyList(from: data, options: [], format: nil)` and reran swift build/self-tests.

error_message: Claude status looked connected even though `claude auth status` returned loggedIn:false
root_cause: Claude health marked the agent ready based on `claude --version` and cached ~/.claude.json account hints. It did not parse `claude auth status`, so a signed-out CLI could appear connected and Run could remain enabled for Claude roles.
fix: Parse `claude auth status`, add an `auth` status displayed as Sign in required with a Connect button, skip live quota polling when signed out, and block task runs when selected roles require a signed-out agent.

error_message: After Claude login succeeds, the app does not automatically fall back/recheck to show the logged-in account
root_cause: The Connect action launches `claude auth login` in Terminal and returns immediately, so `loadAgents()` ran before the user finished browser/terminal authentication. No follow-up polling refreshed the sidebar after credentials landed.
fix: Added post-switch account polling for up to two minutes, plus a sidebar 'Waiting for account...' state that disables account actions while polling. Verified Claude now shows Connected with live quota after login.

error_message: Quota UI hardcoded labels such as 5h/weekly and did not show reset time or account type consistently
root_cause: Claude and Codex parsers inferred display labels from fixed window names or thresholds (`five_hour`, `seven_day`, `window_minutes >= 1440`) instead of rendering the reset timestamp and provider-supplied account type. This breaks for free or other account types with different quota windows.
fix: Render quota windows from payload reset timestamps and dynamic durations, parse numeric epoch reset values, humanize account plan/subscription fields, show a Plan line in the sidebar, and update self-tests to avoid fixed 5h/weekly assertions.

error_message: Quota display is ugly and hard to read because all quota/reset information is shown as one long line
root_cause: AgentStatusRow rendered quota as a single Label string, so multiple windows and reset timestamps had no visual hierarchy and wrapped poorly in the macOS sidebar.
fix: Replaced the single quota Label with QuotaBreakdown: each quota window renders as its own compact block with a percent meter and reset timestamp subline.

error_message: Only Codex quota showed compact window labels like 5h and 7d; Claude quota did not
root_cause: Codex quota labels were derived from window_minutes, but Claude quota labels were humanized from API keys or omitted from reset-bearing rows. That made Claude display less scannable and inconsistent with Codex.
fix: Added a shared quotaWindowLabel helper: use window_minutes when present, otherwise derive compact labels from provider keys such as five_hour -> 5h and seven_day_opus -> 7d Opus. Updated Claude self-tests.

error_message: Release self-test was killed because it was run in parallel with pkill for the Trinity app process
root_cause: The verification command `.build/release/Trinity.app/Contents/MacOS/Trinity --self-test` was grouped in a parallel tool call with `pkill -f Trinity.app/Contents/MacOS/Trinity`, so pkill terminated the self-test binary.
fix: Reran the release self-test separately and recorded the passing result. Do not run app-kill commands in parallel with Trinity binary verification.

error_message: Claude account type showed 'Stripe Subscription' in the UI
root_cause: The Claude fallback account reader used oauthAccount.billingType from ~/.claude.json as the displayed plan. Values such as stripe_subscription describe the payment mechanism, not the user-facing Claude plan.
fix: Stopped using billingType as a Claude plan. The fallback now only reads explicit subscription/plan/tier fields; auth/live usage sources can still provide Pro/Max/etc. Added a self-test so billingType does not surface as an account plan.

error_message: Codex account type showed 'Free' even though the user says the account is not Free
root_cause: Codex status fell back to rate_limits.plan_type and also trusted an id_token ChatGPT plan claim of free. Those values are not reliable enough to present as the user-facing Codex account type.
fix: Stopped using rate_limits.plan_type as Codex account type and suppress the literal free plan claim. Paid plan claims can still display, but unknown/free-like values now omit the plan line instead of inventing a misleading account type.
